intro: Cathartocarpus rhombifolius, also known as the rhombic milk vine or rhombifolius, is a plant species in the family Fabaceae, commonly found in tropical regions. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which can aid in relieving symptoms associated with fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Cathartocarpus rhombifolius include flavonoids, saponins, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, Cathartocarpus rhombifolius is often used to prepare decoctions, infusions, and infusates, as well as being incorporated into herbal teas and tinctures.
